title: Lemma 2(ii): Incomplete justification for continuity of Fisher information

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Moderate Gap in Lemma 2 (lines 116–127)

### The Issue

Lemma 2(ii) asserts: "K_n(T_ε p) → K_n(p) as ε → 0, holding since K_n is continuous in the roots and roots are continuous in ε."

This claim is incomplete because the Fisher information has singularities when roots approach each other.

### Why It Matters

The score vector has terms 1/(α_i - α_j) that diverge as roots coalesce:
- If p has simple roots: K_n(p) is finite, and by continuity of roots, K_n(T_ε p) → K_n(p) (finite limit) ✓
- If p has a multiple root: K_n(p) = ∞ by definition (line 41). As ε → 0, roots of T_ε p approach the coalescence, so differences α_i(ε) - α_j(ε) → 0, causing score terms to blow up and K_n(T_ε p) → ∞

### Missing Justification

The argument should explicitly state:
1. For p with simple roots: Standard continuity applies
2. For p with multiple roots: Use monotone convergence of (sum of squares of diverging terms) to justify K_n(T_ε p) → ∞
3. When taking limits in the reduction (as ε → 0), both cases need to be handled:
   - If K_n(p) is finite, use (ii) for finite limit
   - If K_n(p) = ∞, the inequality becomes 0 ≥ RHS, which is vacuous if RHS ≥ 0

The proof doesn't address how the main inequality extends from case 1 to case 2.

### Suggested Fix

In Lemma 2, split the statement:
- Part (ii.a): For p with simple roots, K_n(T_ε p) → K_n(p) by standard continuity.
- Part (ii.b): For p with multiple roots, K_n(T_ε p) → ∞ as ε → 0.

Then in the main proof (Step 3), explicitly address both cases when extending from simple to general roots.
